column1: How the backend adjusts reps, sets and load from logged performance. Runs at Generator Flow step 9 after every completed workout instance, reading set_logs and writing progression_state. Every adjustment is bounded by the Prescription Grid ranges and the Safety Rules: the engine may move a user within reps_min to reps_max and up to the S07 weekly load cap, and it may never take a user below a floor, above 88 percent 1RM, below 4 reps on a Load exercise, or past the exercise Min RIR. Progression only happens when it is safe: fatigue, missed reps and stop symptoms all pause or reverse it.

column3: Loadable exercise. User hit the top of the rep range (reps_max) with the prescribed RIR or easier, for the required number of sessions (default 2 in a row)
column4: Add one increment: 2.5kg upper body, 5kg lower body, or 5 percent, whichever is smaller. Reset reps to reps_min. Capped by Safety Rule S07 (max 5 percent per week).
column5: progression_state.current_load_kg up, current_rep_target reset to reps_min, sessions_at_current=0, stall_count=0

column3: Non-loadable exercise (bodyweight, band with no more tension), user hit reps_max at target RIR
column4: Add reps beyond reps_max up to a soft ceiling, then slow the lowering phase, then progress to a harder variation via Substitutions (higher skill, same group).
column5: current_rep_target up, or variation swap logged

column3: User completed the session inside the rep range but not at the top, at target RIR
column4: Repeat the same prescription next session. No change.

column3: User missed reps_min, or reported RIR 0 or failure on 2+ sets, or form broke down
column4: Reduce load by 10 percent (loadable) or reps toward reps_min (non-loadable). Rebuild from there. Never drop below the exercise floor.

column3: Same exercise fails to progress for 3 consecutive sessions (stall_count reaches 3)
column4: Trigger a deload on that exercise: cut sets 40 percent and load 10 percent for one session (Safety Rule F01 style), then resume. If it stalls again after the deload, offer a substitute of equal difficulty for variety.

column3: User logs RPE 10 or failure on 3+ sets in a session, or flags poor sleep or a missed prior session
column4: Do not progress this session regardless of PR01/PR02. Apply Safety Rules F02/F03: hold or reduce, raise rir_min by 1. Progression resumes when fatigue clears.
column5: no progression written, fatigue flag noted

column3: An exercise the user has never done (fresh program, or a swap)
column4: Do not guess heavy. Start at an estimate from a similar logged lift if one exists, otherwise start light at RIR 4 to 5 and let the user find the working weight over 2 sessions (Safety Rule P01).
column5: progression_state row created, first 2 sessions marked as calibration

column3: User is in their first 4 weeks
column4: Progress conservatively: rir_min stays at 2, load increments at the bottom of the range, add reps before load. Block Advanced and Power classes (Safety Rule P01).

column1: PR01 says 'add the smallest available increment'. What is available depends on the equipment, not the muscle. The engine rounds the target load to the nearest achievable increment for that equipment before writing it.

column3: Round to nearest 2.5 kg (1.25 kg plate per side). 5 kg jumps for lower-body compounds once past the beginner ramp.
Round to the gym's available dumbbell steps; many go up in 2 kg. If the next dumbbell is too big a jump, add reps first.
Round to one stack plate. If one plate is too large a jump, add reps or use the half-plate pin if present.
Same as machine. Cable stacks often start smaller, so increments are gentler.

column2: add reps, then tempo, then harder variation
column3: No external load. Progress via PR02, not PR01.
Discrete jumps; add reps within a band before moving up a level, the jump between bands is large.
Less assistance means more of your own bodyweight. Progress by removing assistance, not adding load.

column1: Non-weight progression: duration exercises progress by seconds up to the Time cap (60s), interval exercises by rounds then work-interval length, carries by distance or time, assisted exercises by reducing assistance. The engine reads measurement_type to know which value to move.
